commands.repair_reason: - Protected maintainer/draft PR with a security-boundary decision; no automated repair is indicated unless maintainers request a different implementation path.; Needs attention: No injection bug is evident because the shell command is static and token data stays on stdin, but the new shell process is a concrete security-boundary change requiring maintainer acceptance. (sha=c51ccd58c3ef3c917c889ea7de431f57e2ba60ec)
commands.review_summary:
The PR prepends a WSL2-only static `/bin/sh -c` bridge to the clipboard backend and adds tests asserting dashboard token values stay on stdin while non-WSL fallback order remains unchanged.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +41. Total +47 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. with medium confidence from source: current main still calls direct `clip.exe` for clipboard fallback, while the linked report describes WSL2 `execFile`/spawn failure and this PR supplies after-fix WSL2 readback proof. I did not run a live current-main WSL2 before/after repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - Protected maintainer/draft PR with a security-boundary decision; no automated repair is indicated unless maintainers request a different implementation path.

target.title: fix(infra): bridge WSL clipboard through shell
target.body:
## Summary

- route WSL2 clipboard copies through a static `/bin/sh -c` bridge to `/mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe`
- keep dashboard/token URLs on stdin instead of shell argv, and avoid Bash login/profile startup
- preserve the existing macOS, Linux, native Windows, PowerShell, and non-WSL2 fallback order
- tighten the regression test so token substrings cannot hide inside argv elements

Fixes #88080.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clipboard.test.ts src/infra/wsl.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.test.ts` - passed after final rebase, 34 focused tests
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean after final rebase, no accepted/actionable findings
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target windows --windows-mode wsl2 --no-hydrate --no-sync --label wsl-clipboard-88365-readback --idle-timeout 30m --ttl 60m --timing-json --stop-after always --script-stdin` - passed on AWS Windows/WSL2 Crabbox, run `run_d9aa824b1545`; generated probe wrote through `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and matched `Get-Clipboard`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target windows --windows-mode normal --no-hydrate --no-sync --label native-windows-clipboard-88365-readback-script --idle-timeout 30m --ttl 60m --timing-json --stop-after always --script-stdin` - passed on AWS native Windows Crabbox, run `run_240b0da09546`; generated probe wrote through direct `%SystemRoot%\System32\clip.exe` and matched `Get-Clipboard`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --label wsl-clipboard-88365-check-changed --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --stop-after always --shell -- 'pnpm check:changed'` - blocked on AWS Linux Crabbox, run `run_e27c4826ef31`, by current-main `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts(165,5)` boundary dts error before touched-file lint; changed-gate core/core-test typecheck and earlier guards passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WSL2 dashboard clipboard copies no longer rely on direct non-shell `clip.exe` argv execution or a Bash login shell. The WSL2 path uses `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and passes the copied dashboard URL through stdin.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Windows/WSL2 host for shell-bridge clipboard write/readback; AWS Crabbox native Windows host for direct Windows clipboard write/readback; AWS Crabbox Linux for `pnpm check:changed`; focused local Vitest/format/lint/diff-check; branch autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest, oxfmt, touched-file oxlint, diff-check, branch autoreview, AWS Windows/WSL2 clipboard readback, AWS native Windows clipboard readback, and AWS Linux `pnpm check:changed` commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: the Windows/WSL2 run wrote a generated probe string through `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and read it back with Windows PowerShell `Get-Clipboard`; expected and actual values matched exactly. The native Windows run wrote a generated probe through direct `%SystemRoot%\System32\clip.exe` and read it back with `Get-Clipboard`; expected and actual values matched. The WSL2 regression test asserts copied values stay on stdin rather than argv, and the non-WSL fallback-order test keeps the existing `pbcopy`, `xclip`, `wl-copy`, `clip.exe`, and PowerShell order.

Observed result after fix: Windows/WSL2 clipboard readback passed on commit `c51ccd58c3ef3c917c889ea7de431f57e2ba60ec`; native Windows clipboard readback passed; focused tests, touched-file format/lint, diff-check, and autoreview passed. `pnpm check:changed` reached core/core-test typecheck successfully and then failed at the known current-main Discord boundary dts issue outside this branch.

What was not tested: `openclaw dashboard --no-open` end-to-end with a real token URL on a user workstation. The runner proof directly exercised the WSL2 clipboard bridge that dashboard calls, using non-secret generated probe values.
